  Inode:                 21654802
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/html
  SHA-256:               de750777b00d3e98cfcc52d44532688ffda0e411f5cf9e1759747a50bd4e202f
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
@page "/access-denied"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]

@* Where the application cookie's AccessDeniedPath lands (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.7): an
   authenticated principal asked for an area their roles do not grant. Interactive pages show the
   same denial inline via the router's NotAuthorized branch; this page covers server-enforced
   (static SSR / endpoint) authorization. Exempt from the obligations pipeline so a flagged user who
   trips a policy is not redirect-looped. *@

<PageTitle>Access denied</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">Access denied</p>
    <h1>You do not have access to this area</h1>

    <AuthorizeView>
        <Authorized>
            <p class="lede">
                You are signed in as <strong>@context.User.Identity?.Name</strong>, but this area
                requires a role your account does not hold. If you believe you should have it, an
                administrator can grant the role.
            </p>
        </Authorized>
        <NotAuthorized>
            <p class="lede">This area requires a role your session does not hold.</p>
        </NotAuthorized>
    </AuthorizeView>

    <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
</section>

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/ChangePasswordRequired.razor
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             ChangePasswordRequired.razor
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/ChangePasswordRequired.razor
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/ChangePasswordRequired.razor
  Size:                  6.5 KiB (6611 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 21:14:34.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-------
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21675158
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               912c578b97ae1262965aecb6152455c48e683a76a47646fd32b7869aff5f5387
  Last git commit:       a4423d9 2026-07-19 17:19:43 -0400 add claude response

--- CONTENT ---
@page "/account/change-password-required"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize]
@using MyRestaurant.Domain.Authentication
@inject UserManager<Person> UserManager
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject ISecurityEventLog SecurityEventLog
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Obligation (1) of the post-authentication pipeline (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.5, ADR-0010): an
   administrative reset set `must_change_password`, and ObligationsMiddleware routes every request
   here until it clears. The flag is cleared in the SAME store update that persists the new hash
   (set before ChangePasswordAsync; only its success path reaches UpdateAsync), a
   `forced_password_change_completed` event is recorded, and the cookie is refreshed so the cleared
   obligation claim takes effect immediately. Static SSR: the refreshed cookie must ride the response. *@

<PageTitle>Choose a new password</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">Action required</p>
    <h1>Choose a new password</h1>
    <p class="lede">
        An administrator reset this account, so the temporary password you just used must be
        replaced before you can continue. Pick something at least 12 characters long — length is the
        only rule, so a short sentence works well.
    </p>

    @if (_errors.Count > 0)
    {
        <ul class="status-error" role="alert">
            @foreach (string error in _errors)
            {
                <li>@error</li>
            }
        </ul>
    }

    <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="ChangePasswordAsync" FormName="change-password-required">
        <DataAnnotationsValidator />

        <div class="form-field">
            <label for="current-password">Temporary (current) password</label>
            <InputText id="current-password" type="password" @bind-Value="Input.CurrentPassword"
                       autocomplete="current-password" autofocus />
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.CurrentPassword)" />
        </div>

        <div class="form-field">
            <label for="new-password">New password</label>
            <InputText id="new-password" type="password" @bind-Value="Input.NewPassword"
                       autocomplete="new-password" />
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.NewPassword)" />
        </div>

        <div class="form-field">
            <label for="confirm-password">New password, again</label>
            <InputText id="confirm-password" type="password" @bind-Value="Input.ConfirmPassword"
                       autocomplete="new-password" />
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.ConfirmPassword)" />
        </div>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Set new password</button>
        </div>
    </EditForm>

    <form class="sign-out-form form-footnote" method="post" action="@AccountRoutes.SignOut">
        <AntiforgeryToken />
        <span>Not ready right now?</span>
        <button type="submit" class="link-button">Sign out</button>
    </form>
</section>

@code {
    private readonly List<string> _errors = [];

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private ChangePasswordInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here
        // rather than in a property initializer the binder would silently discard.
        Input ??= new();

        Person? user = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (user is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        // Nothing outstanding (stale link, or the flag cleared in another tab): move along. The
        // database is consulted rather than the claim so a just-cleared flag never re-traps anyone.
        if (!user.MustChangePassword && HttpContext.Request.Method == HttpMethods.Get)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl));
        }
    }

    private async Task ChangePasswordAsync()
    {
        Person? user = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (user is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        string destination = ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl);

        if (!user.MustChangePassword)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(destination);
            return;
        }

        // Clear the flag on the entity BEFORE the change: ChangePasswordAsync persists the entity
        // (UpdateAsync) only on its success path, so the new hash and the cleared flag commit in one
        // store update — and a failed attempt persists nothing.
        user.MustChangePassword = false;
        IdentityResult result = await UserManager.ChangePasswordAsync(
            user,
            Input.CurrentPassword,
            Input.NewPassword);

        if (!result.Succeeded)
        {
            user.MustChangePassword = true;
            _errors.Clear();
            _errors.AddRange(result.Errors.Select(error => error.Description));
            return;
        }

        await SecurityEventLog.RecordAsync(
            user.PersonIdentifier,
            actorPersonIdentifier: null,
            SecurityEventType.ForcedPasswordChangeCompleted);

        // Re-issue the cookie: fresh security stamp, and the obligation claim disappears with it, so
        // the middleware releases this principal on the very next request.
        await SignInManager.RefreshSignInAsync(user);

        Navigation.NavigateTo(destination);
    }

    private sealed class ChangePasswordInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter the temporary password you signed in with.")]
        public string CurrentPassword { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Choose a new password.")]
        [MinLength(12, ErrorMessage = "Passwords are at least 12 characters (§3.2) — a short sentence works.")]
        public string NewPassword { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Repeat the new password.")]
        [Compare(nameof(NewPassword), ErrorMessage = "The two entries do not match.")]
        public string ConfirmPassword { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--

  Inode:                 21654799
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               97003bbc1d004783b799441071c5698239705ffde0f0a8cbddcb76b01c26efa8
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
@page "/account/enroll-totp-required"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@attribute [Authorize]
@inject UserManager<Person> UserManager
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Obligation (2) of the post-authentication pipeline (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.5): forced TOTP
   re-enrollment after an administrative reset wiped an enrolled secret. The enrollment mechanics
   (provisioning URI → server-side SVG QR → confirm code → fresh recovery codes → clear flag →
   `forced_totp_enrollment_completed`) are the next M2 slice; this page is its addressable, exempt
   HOME so the middleware, routes, and tests are final now. Nothing in the application can set
   `must_enroll_totp` yet — administrative reset arrives in a later slice — so in practice this page
   is only reachable by hand-editing the database (BUILD_PROGRESS: deliberate stub). *@

<PageTitle>Re-enroll your authenticator</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">Action required</p>
    <h1>Re-enroll your authenticator</h1>
    <p class="lede">
        An administrator reset this account, which wiped its previous authenticator enrollment and
        recovery codes. A fresh enrollment (QR code, confirmation, new recovery codes) is required
        before you can continue.
    </p>
    <p class="lede">
        <strong>The enrollment screen is not built yet</strong> — it arrives with the TOTP milestone
        slice. Until then this account stays parked here; an administrator can clear the requirement
        directly if it is blocking someone.
    </p>

    <form class="sign-out-form form-footnote" method="post" action="@AccountRoutes.SignOut">
        <AntiforgeryToken />
        <span>Done for now?</span>
        <button type="submit" class="link-button">Sign out</button>
    </form>
</section>

@code {
    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        Person? user = await UserManager.GetUserAsync(HttpContext.User);
        if (user is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        // Nothing outstanding: pass the visitor along instead of showing a stale demand.
        if (!user.MustEnrollTotp)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl));
        }
    }
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignIn.razor
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             SignIn.razor
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignIn.razor
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignIn.razor
  Size:                  4.4 KiB (4508 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 21:14:34.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-------
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21675159
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/html
  SHA-256:               7ca49793281d6e672264a3d1dbd2fadfbb530085c934ef0209a4457c1c08df00
  Last git commit:       a4423d9 2026-07-19 17:19:43 -0400 add claude response

--- CONTENT ---
@page "/sign-in"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* The password sign-in page (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.5, §4.1). Static SSR on purpose: issuing the
   authentication cookie needs a real HTTP response, which a Blazor circuit does not have. The form
   is a plain full-page POST (EditForm in SSR emits the antiforgery token itself). Auditing and
   metering happen centrally in RestaurantSignInManager, never here. *@

<PageTitle>Sign in</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">MyRestaurant</p>
    <h1>Sign in</h1>

    @if (HttpContext.User.Identity?.IsAuthenticated == true)
    {
        <p class="lede">
            You are already signed in as <strong>@HttpContext.User.Identity?.Name</strong>.
        </p>
        <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
    }
    else
    {
        @if (_errorMessage is not null)
        {
            <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
        }

        <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="SignInUserAsync" FormName="sign-in">
            <DataAnnotationsValidator />

            <div class="form-field">
                <label for="username">Username</label>
                <InputText id="username" @bind-Value="Input.Username" autocomplete="username"
                           autocapitalize="none" spellcheck="false" autofocus />
                <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Username)" />
            </div>

            <div class="form-field">
                <label for="password">Password</label>
                <InputText id="password" type="password" @bind-Value="Input.Password"
                           autocomplete="current-password" />
                <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Password)" />
            </div>

            <div class="form-actions">
                <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Sign in</button>
            </div>
        </EditForm>

        <p class="form-footnote">
            Guests register at the table by scanning its display — there is no stand-alone
            registration page. Lost access? Ask at the counter.
        </p>
    }
</section>

@code {
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private SignInInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    // BL0008: on a static-SSR form POST the framework binds [SupplyParameterFromForm] itself and may
    // assign null, silently discarding a `= new()` initializer — so the default is applied here instead.
    protected override void OnInitialized() => Input ??= new();

    private async Task SignInUserAsync()
    {
        string destination = ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl);

        // isPersistent: the §3.1 session is a 24-hour sliding cookie either way; persisting it lets
        // a guest's phone survive a browser restart mid-meal. lockoutOnFailure per §3.1.
        SignInResult result = await SignInManager.PasswordSignInAsync(
            Input.Username,
            Input.Password,
            isPersistent: true,
            lockoutOnFailure: true);

        if (result.Succeeded)
        {
            // The obligations middleware intercepts this navigation when a §3.5 flag is set.
            Navigation.NavigateTo(destination);
        }
        else if (result.RequiresTwoFactor)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(
                $"{AccountRoutes.SignInTwoFactor}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(destination)}");
        }
        else if (result.IsLockedOut)
        {
            _errorMessage = "Too many failed attempts. The account is locked for about five minutes — try again then.";
        }
        else if (result.IsNotAllowed)
        {
            _errorMessage = "This account has been deactivated. Ask at the counter if you think that is a mistake.";
        }
        else
        {
            _errorMessage = "That username and password combination did not work.";
        }
    }

    private sealed class SignInInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter your username.")]
        public string Username { get; set; } = string.Empty;

        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter your password.")]
        public string Password { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignInRecoveryCode.razor

--- METADATA ---

  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignInRecoveryCode.razor

  Last modified:         2026-07-19 21:14:34.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-------

  Inode:                 21675160
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/html
  SHA-256:               a9cadc1e700284c1579ccd9da2e35d20546a611c77a5b227defa3cf9a0a992f2
  Last git commit:       a4423d9 2026-07-19 17:19:43 -0400 add claude response

--- CONTENT ---
@page "/sign-in/recovery-code"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* A recovery code standing in for the TOTP code on the password path (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.4).
   Each code is single-use; a successful redemption is recorded (`recovery_code_used`) by
   RestaurantSignInManager. Like the TOTP page, this is only reachable mid-sign-in. *@

<PageTitle>Recovery code</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">MyRestaurant</p>
    <h1>Use a recovery code</h1>
    <p class="lede">
        Enter one of the recovery codes you saved when you enrolled your authenticator. Each code
        works exactly once — when you are back in, consider regenerating a fresh set from your
        profile.
    </p>

    @if (_errorMessage is not null)
    {
        <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
    }

    <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="RedeemCodeAsync" FormName="sign-in-recovery-code">
        <DataAnnotationsValidator />

        <div class="form-field">
            <label for="recovery-code">Recovery code</label>
            <InputText id="recovery-code" @bind-Value="Input.RecoveryCode" autocomplete="off"
                       autocapitalize="none" spellcheck="false" autofocus />
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.RecoveryCode)" />
        </div>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Sign in</button>
        </div>
    </EditForm>

    <p class="form-footnote">
        Found your authenticator after all?
        <a href="@TwoFactorHref">Enter the six-digit code instead.</a>
    </p>
</section>

@code {
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private RecoveryCodeInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    private string TwoFactorHref
        => $"{AccountRoutes.SignInTwoFactor}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl))}";

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here
        // rather than in a property initializer the binder would silently discard.
        Input ??= new();

        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
        }
    }

    private async Task RedeemCodeAsync()
    {
        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        SignInResult result = await SignInManager.TwoFactorRecoveryCodeSignInAsync(Input.RecoveryCode.Trim());

        if (result.Succeeded)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl));
        }
        else if (result.IsLockedOut)
        {
            _errorMessage = "Too many failed attempts. The account is locked for about five minutes — try again then.";
        }
        else
        {
            _errorMessage = "That recovery code did not work. Codes are single-use — try another from your saved set.";
        }
    }

    private sealed class RecoveryCodeInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter a recovery code.")]
        public string RecoveryCode { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignInTwoFactor.razor
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             SignInTwoFactor.razor
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignInTwoFactor.razor
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/Pages/SignInTwoFactor.razor
  Size:                  3.8 KiB (3898 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 21:14:34.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-------
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21675161
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/html
  SHA-256:               bd5815df9ea5328464275fc978cb73f5ac379b159a3d6067d9e117795fbb217a
  Last git commit:       a4423d9 2026-07-19 17:19:43 -0400 add claude response

--- CONTENT ---
@page "/sign-in/two-factor"
@attribute [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
@inject SignInManager<Person> SignInManager
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* The TOTP challenge on the password path (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.4, §4.2, ADR-0010). Reachable
   only mid-sign-in: the password step parked the pending user in the two-factor cookie; without it
   this page bounces back to /sign-in. There is deliberately no "remember this device" — TOTP is
   challenged on every password sign-in of an enrolled account. The passkey path never lands here. *@

<PageTitle>Two-factor code</PageTitle>

<section class="panel account-panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">MyRestaurant</p>
    <h1>Enter your authenticator code</h1>
    <p class="lede">
        Your password was correct. Open your authenticator app and enter the six-digit code for this
        account.
    </p>

    @if (_errorMessage is not null)
    {
        <p class="status-error" role="alert">@_errorMessage</p>
    }

    <EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="VerifyCodeAsync" FormName="sign-in-two-factor">
        <DataAnnotationsValidator />

        <div class="form-field">
            <label for="totp-code">Authenticator code</label>
            <InputText id="totp-code" @bind-Value="Input.Code" autocomplete="one-time-code"
                       inputmode="numeric" autofocus />
            <ValidationMessage For="@(() => Input.Code)" />
        </div>

        <div class="form-actions">
            <button type="submit" class="button-primary">Verify</button>
        </div>
    </EditForm>

    <p class="form-footnote">
        Authenticator unavailable?
        <a href="@RecoveryCodeHref">Use a recovery code instead.</a>
    </p>
</section>

@code {
    private string? _errorMessage;

    [SupplyParameterFromForm]
    private TwoFactorInputModel Input { get; set; } = default!;

    [SupplyParameterFromQuery]
    private string? ReturnUrl { get; set; }

    private string RecoveryCodeHref
        => $"{AccountRoutes.SignInRecoveryCode}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl))}";

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        // BL0008: a form POST binds Input itself and may assign null, so the default lives here
        // rather than in a property initializer the binder would silently discard.
        Input ??= new();

        // No pending two-factor user means this page was reached out of order — start over.
        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
        }
    }

    private async Task VerifyCodeAsync()
    {
        if (await SignInManager.GetTwoFactorAuthenticationUserAsync() is null)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(AccountRoutes.SignIn);
            return;
        }

        // Authenticator apps display codes in groups; be forgiving about spaces and dashes.
        string code = Input.Code.Replace(" ", string.Empty).Replace("-", string.Empty);

        SignInResult result = await SignInManager.TwoFactorAuthenticatorSignInAsync(
            code,
            isPersistent: true,
            rememberClient: false);

        if (result.Succeeded)
        {
            Navigation.NavigateTo(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(ReturnUrl));
        }
        else if (result.IsLockedOut)
        {
            _errorMessage = "Too many failed attempts. The account is locked for about five minutes — try again then.";
        }
        else
        {
            _errorMessage = "That code did not verify. Codes rotate every 30 seconds — check the app and try the current one.";
        }
    }

    private sealed class TwoFactorInputModel
    {
        [Required(ErrorMessage = "Enter the six-digit code.")]
        public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    }
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/RedirectToSignIn.razor
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             RedirectToSignIn.razor
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/RedirectToSignIn.razor
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Account/RedirectToSignIn.razor
  Size:                  724 B (724 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 08:46:36.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21654803
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               d34f1240708b7df0502a74fc2025803e804e32a47ebdb494f7c0eaa871a2ba06
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
@inject NavigationManager Navigation

@* Sends an anonymous visitor to the sign-in page, preserving where they were headed. forceLoad is
   required: the sign-in page is excluded from interactive routing (static SSR), so the interactive
   router cannot navigate to it client-side — a full document load is the only correct transition.
   During static prerendering the NavigateTo call surfaces as an HTTP redirect instead. *@

@code {
    protected override void OnInitialized()
    {
        string destination = "/" + Navigation.ToBaseRelativePath(Navigation.Uri);
        Navigation.NavigateTo(
            $"{AccountRoutes.SignIn}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(destination)}",
            forceLoad: true);
    }
}

  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/html
  SHA-256:               37515d5ed875a03c37b63cb2d5a45c9e541ab376ece542f50d36a902625d194d
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
@* The root HTML document. Interactive-server rendering is the default for the whole app (ADR-0004),
   but it is applied per page rather than hard-coded: pages marked [ExcludeFromInteractiveRouting]
   (the account/sign-in surfaces) render as static SSR instead, because writing the authentication
   cookie requires a real HTTP response — a Blazor circuit cannot set cookies (BUILD_PROGRESS:
   "sign-in pages must post to a static/SSR endpoint"). *@
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <base href="/" />
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="app.css" />
    <link rel="icon" href="data:," />
    <HeadOutlet @rendermode="RenderModeForPage" />
</head>

<body>
    <Routes @rendermode="RenderModeForPage" />
    <script src="_framework/blazor.web.js"></script>
</body>

</html>

@code {
    [CascadingParameter]
    private HttpContext HttpContext { get; set; } = default!;

    /// <summary>
    /// Interactive server for ordinary pages; <c>null</c> (static SSR) for pages that opted out with
    /// <see cref="ExcludeFromInteractiveRoutingAttribute"/> — the sign-in and obligation pages.
    /// </summary>
    private IComponentRenderMode? RenderModeForPage
        => HttpContext.AcceptsInteractiveRouting() ? InteractiveServer : null;
}

@inherits LayoutComponentBase

@* The shared shell. The session header uses a plain HTML form for sign-out — a full-page POST to
   the /sign-out endpoint with the antiforgery token — so it behaves identically whether the hosting
   page renders as static SSR (the account pages) or over an interactive circuit. *@
<div class="app-shell">
    <header class="app-header">
        <a class="app-brand" href="/">MyRestaurant</a>
        <nav class="app-session" aria-label="Session">
            <AuthorizeView>
                <Authorized>
                    <span class="session-name">@context.User.Identity?.Name</span>
                    <form class="sign-out-form" method="post" action="@AccountRoutes.SignOut">
                        <AntiforgeryToken />
                        <button type="submit" class="link-button">Sign out</button>
                    </form>
                </Authorized>
                <NotAuthorized>
                    <a class="session-link" href="@AccountRoutes.SignIn">Sign in</a>
                </NotAuthorized>
            </AuthorizeView>
        </nav>
    </header>

    <main class="app-main">
        @Body
    </main>
</div>

    <h1 class="landing-title">@Options.RestaurantName</h1>

    <p class="landing-lede">
        The application shell is running. The Milestone&nbsp;1 skeleton (domain, data access, web
        host, startup migrations) is in place, and Milestone&nbsp;2 is under way: password sign-in
        with the TOTP/recovery challenge, lockout, and the forced-password-change pipeline work now.

        milestones.

        <a href="/table">Table area (sign-in required)</a>

@page "/table"
@attribute [Authorize(Policy = AuthorizationPolicies.Table)]

@* The first [Authorize]-gated area (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.7 — `area.table`: any authenticated
   person). This interactive placeholder exists to prove the whole chain live — cookie → claims →
   policy → AuthorizeRouteView → circuit — before the real guest experience (joining, staging,
   sending; M3/M4) replaces its body. Anonymous visitors are redirected to /sign-in with this page
   as the return URL. *@

<PageTitle>Your table</PageTitle>

<section class="panel">
    <p class="eyebrow">Table area</p>
    <h1>Signed in and gated</h1>

    <AuthorizeView>
        <Authorized>
            <p class="lede">
                You are signed in as <strong>@context.User.Identity?.Name</strong>@DisplayNameSuffix(context.User),
                and the <code>area.table</code> policy admitted you — the authentication cookie, the
                claims principal, and the area authorization are all live.
            </p>
        </Authorized>
    </AuthorizeView>

    <p class="lede">
        Joining a table by scanning its rotating code, and building and sending orders, arrive with
        Milestones&nbsp;3 and&nbsp;4.
    </p>

    <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
</section>

@code {
    private static string DisplayNameSuffix(System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal user)
    {
        string? displayName = user.FindFirst(RestaurantClaimTypes.DisplayName)?.Value;
        return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(displayName) ? string.Empty : $" ({displayName})";
    }
}

# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Routes.razor
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             Routes.razor
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Routes.razor
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Components/Routes.razor
  Size:                  1.6 KiB (1636 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 08:46:12.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21654788
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/html
  SHA-256:               1af42e54c1f9d204bb4e19d3f21338a98cd48073cb8e2d8f8c334d64ecf8d4a2
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
@* Top-level router. AuthorizeRouteView enforces [Authorize] on interactive pages: an anonymous
   visitor is sent to the sign-in page with the destination preserved, while an authenticated
   principal that fails an area policy (§3.7) sees the denial where they stand. The cascading
   authentication state comes from AddCascadingAuthenticationState (wired in AddRestaurantIdentity),
   so this works in both static SSR and interactive-server rendering. Static-SSR pages carrying
   [Authorize] are additionally enforced by endpoint authorization on the server. *@
<Router AppAssembly="typeof(Program).Assembly">
    <Found Context="routeData">
        <AuthorizeRouteView RouteData="routeData" DefaultLayout="typeof(MainLayout)">
            <NotAuthorized>
                @if (context.User.Identity?.IsAuthenticated == true)
                {
                    <section class="panel">
                        <p class="eyebrow">Access denied</p>
                        <h1>You do not have access to this area</h1>
                        <p class="lede">
                            You are signed in as <strong>@context.User.Identity?.Name</strong>, but this
                            area requires a role your account does not hold.
                        </p>
                        <p><a href="/">Back to the home page</a></p>
                    </section>
                }
                else
                {
                    <RedirectToSignIn />
                }
            </NotAuthorized>
        </AuthorizeRouteView>
        <FocusOnNavigate RouteData="routeData" Selector="h1" />
    </Found>
</Router>

# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Configuration/RestaurantOptions.cs
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             RestaurantOptions.cs
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Configuration/RestaurantOptions.cs
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Configuration/RestaurantOptions.cs
  Size:                  7.5 KiB (7703 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-17 20:12:37.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21258197
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               66a7b3b943e87edd34b6e4b3f64789783b2756579a3ae18283f3f4669112c20e
  Last git commit:       21c2eac 2026-07-18 01:04:22 -0400 add M1

--- CONTENT ---
using System.Globalization;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Configuration;

/// <summary>
/// The complete environment-only configuration (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §13; REQUIREMENTS §8:
/// "environment variables only"). Bound once at startup from the flat, long-named,
/// prefixed variables, then <see cref="Validate"/> is run before HTTP is bound so the process
/// fails fast on invalid security-relevant configuration (§13, §3.2 floor guard).
/// </summary>
public sealed class RestaurantOptions
{
    // Argon2 floor guard (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.2). Below any of these the process must not start.
    public const int MinimumArgon2MemoryKibibytes = 19456;
    public const int MinimumArgon2Iterations = 2;
    public const int MinimumArgon2Parallelism = 1;

    // Token/grant/pairing lower bounds (§13: "rotation/grant/pairing values ≥ 10 s / ≥ 1 min / ≥ 1 min").
    public const int MinimumTableJoinTokenRotationSeconds = 10;
    public const int MinimumTableJoinGrantMinutes = 1;
    public const int MinimumTableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes = 1;

    public required string RestaurantName { get; init; }
    public required string PublicOrigin { get; init; }
    public required string TimeZoneId { get; init; }
    public required string CurrencyCode { get; init; }
    public required string DatabaseConnectionString { get; init; }
    public required string DataProtectionKeysDirectory { get; init; }
    public required int KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds { get; init; }
    public required int TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds { get; init; }
    public required int TableJoinGrantMinutes { get; init; }
    public required int TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2MemoryKibibytes { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2Iterations { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2Parallelism { get; init; }
    public required int Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes { get; init; }

    public static RestaurantOptions FromConfiguration(IConfiguration configuration)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(configuration);

        return new RestaurantOptions
        {
            RestaurantName = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_NAME", "My Restaurant"),
            PublicOrigin = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN", "https://localhost:8443"),
            TimeZoneId = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_TIME_ZONE", "America/New_York"),
            CurrencyCode = ReadString(configuration, "RESTAURANT_CURRENCY_CODE", "USD"),
            DatabaseConnectionString = ReadString(
                configuration,
                "RESTAURANT_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING",
                "Host=localhost;Port=5432;Database=myrestaurant;Username=myrestaurant;Password=myrestaurant"),
            DataProtectionKeysDirectory = ReadString(configuration, "DATA_PROTECTION_KEYS_DIRECTORY", "/var/lib/myrestaurant/dataprotection"),
            KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds = ReadInt(configuration, "KITCHEN_SUBMISSION_REMINDER_SECONDS", 60),
            TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds = ReadInt(configuration, "TABLE_JOIN_TOKEN_ROTATION_SECONDS", 60),
            TableJoinGrantMinutes = ReadInt(configuration, "TABLE_JOIN_GRANT_MINUTES", 10),
            TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes = ReadInt(configuration, "TABLE_DISPLAY_PAIRING_CODE_MINUTES", 10),
            Argon2MemoryKibibytes = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_MEMORY_KIBIBYTES", 65536),
            Argon2Iterations = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_ITERATIONS", 3),
            Argon2Parallelism = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_PARALLELISM", 1),
            Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes = ReadInt(configuration, "ARGON2_MAX_CONCURRENT_HASHES", 4),
        };
    }

    /// <summary>Returns a human-readable reason for every invalid setting; empty means valid.</summary>
    public IReadOnlyList<string> Validate()
    {
        List<string> errors = [];

        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(DatabaseConnectionString))
        {
            errors.Add("RESTAURANT_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING must be set.");
        }

        if (!Uri.TryCreate(PublicOrigin, UriKind.Absolute, out Uri? origin)
            || !string.Equals(origin.Scheme, Uri.UriSchemeHttps, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        {
            errors.Add($"RESTAURANT_PUBLIC_ORIGIN must be an absolute https URL (was '{PublicOrigin}').");
        }

        if (!TryResolveTimeZone(TimeZoneId))
        {
            errors.Add($"RESTAURANT_TIME_ZONE '{TimeZoneId}' is not a resolvable time zone on this host.");
        }

        if (CurrencyCode.Length != 3 || !CurrencyCode.All(char.IsAsciiLetter))
        {
            errors.Add($"RESTAURANT_CURRENCY_CODE must be a 3-letter ISO 4217 code (was '{CurrencyCode}').");
        }

        if (Argon2MemoryKibibytes < MinimumArgon2MemoryKibibytes)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_MEMORY_KIBIBYTES must be at least {MinimumArgon2MemoryKibibytes} (was {Argon2MemoryKibibytes}).");
        }

        if (Argon2Iterations < MinimumArgon2Iterations)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_ITERATIONS must be at least {MinimumArgon2Iterations} (was {Argon2Iterations}).");
        }

        if (Argon2Parallelism < MinimumArgon2Parallelism)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_PARALLELISM must be at least {MinimumArgon2Parallelism} (was {Argon2Parallelism}).");
        }

        if (Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes < 1)
        {
            errors.Add($"ARGON2_MAX_CONCURRENT_HASHES must be at least 1 (was {Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes}).");
        }

        if (TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds < MinimumTableJoinTokenRotationSeconds)
        {
            errors.Add($"TABLE_JOIN_TOKEN_ROTATION_SECONDS must be at least {MinimumTableJoinTokenRotationSeconds} (was {TableJoinTokenRotationSeconds}).");
        }

        if (TableJoinGrantMinutes < MinimumTableJoinGrantMinutes)
        {
            errors.Add($"TABLE_JOIN_GRANT_MINUTES must be at least {MinimumTableJoinGrantMinutes} (was {TableJoinGrantMinutes}).");
        }

        if (TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes < MinimumTableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes)
        {
            errors.Add($"TABLE_DISPLAY_PAIRING_CODE_MINUTES must be at least {MinimumTableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes} (was {TableDisplayPairingCodeMinutes}).");
        }

        if (KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds < 1)
        {
            errors.Add($"KITCHEN_SUBMISSION_REMINDER_SECONDS must be at least 1 (was {KitchenSubmissionReminderSeconds}).");
        }

        return errors;
    }

    /// <summary>The WebAuthn relying-party identifier: the full host of the public origin (§3.3).</summary>
    public string ResolveWebAuthnRelyingPartyId() => new Uri(PublicOrigin).Host;

    /// <summary>The configured display time zone (validated at startup).</summary>
    public TimeZoneInfo ResolveTimeZone() => TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById(TimeZoneId);

    private static bool TryResolveTimeZone(string timeZoneId)
    {
        try
        {
            _ = TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById(timeZoneId);
            return true;
        }
        catch (Exception exception) when (exception is TimeZoneNotFoundException or InvalidTimeZoneException)
        {
            return false;
        }
    }

    private static string ReadString(IConfiguration configuration, string key, string fallback)
    {
        string? value = configuration[key];
        return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) ? fallback : value;
    }

    private static int ReadInt(IConfiguration configuration, string key, int fallback)
        => int.TryParse(configuration[key], NumberStyles.Integer, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out int value)
            ? value
            : fallback;
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/AccountEndpoints.cs
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             AccountEndpoints.cs
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/AccountEndpoints.cs
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/AccountEndpoints.cs
  Size:                  1.7 KiB (1748 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 08:44:05.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21654782
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               9d8b21f7117a473ee058b8d25aa7461d70bae416f1eb72678e11370e39a0d261
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;
using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Identity;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Identity;

/// <summary>
/// The non-page account endpoints. Sign-out is a POST endpoint rather than a page
/// (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.5): it must clear the authentication cookies on the response, must
/// never be triggerable by a crafted GET link, and must stay reachable while the obligations
/// pipeline blocks everything else (its path is exempt in <see cref="ObligationsEnforcement"/>).
/// Binding the optional form field makes the endpoint "accept form data", which switches on the
/// framework's automatic antiforgery validation for it — the layout's sign-out form supplies the
/// token via <c>&lt;AntiforgeryToken /&gt;</c>.
/// </summary>
public static class AccountEndpoints
{
    public static IEndpointRouteBuilder MapRestaurantAccountEndpoints(this IEndpointRouteBuilder endpoints)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(endpoints);

        endpoints.MapPost(AccountRoutes.SignOut, async (
            SignInManager<Person> signInManager,
            [FromForm] string? returnUrl) =>
        {
            // Clears the application, external, and both two-factor cookies. Harmless when the
            // caller was already anonymous. No security_event is written: sign-out is not in the
            // §8.2 event-type vocabulary (sessions also end silently by expiry and stamp rotation,
            // so recording only explicit sign-outs would tell a misleading story).
            await signInManager.SignOutAsync();
            return Results.LocalRedirect(ObligationsEnforcement.SafeLocalReturnUrl(returnUrl));
        });

        return endpoints;
    }
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/IdentityServiceCollectionExtensions.cs
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---
  File name:             IdentityServiceCollectionExtensions.cs
  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/IdentityServiceCollectionExtensions.cs
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/IdentityServiceCollectionExtensions.cs
  Size:                  8.9 KiB (9085 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 08:44:41.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21654780
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               75d22f4073764b55ca846660ac9c249f4279fb36d5101e64958934787bf24f4c
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.Cookies;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Extensions;
using MyRestaurant.DataAccess.Identity;
using MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Authorization;
using MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Configuration;
using MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Observability;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Identity;

/// <summary>
/// Wires ASP.NET Core Identity <b>core</b> services (never the EF default stores/UI) over the custom
/// Dapper store, with the Argon2id hasher replacing Identity's PBKDF2 default
/// (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.1–§3.2, ADR-0003/ADR-0008), plus sign-in and authorization:
/// <list type="bullet">
///   <item>the Identity cookie scheme, hardened (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, 24-hour sliding),
///   pointing at the sign-in / sign-out / access-denied surfaces in <see cref="AccountRoutes"/>;</item>
///   <item><see cref="RestaurantSignInManager"/> (the auditing <see cref="SignInManager{TUser}"/>,
///   which also refuses deactivated accounts);</item>
///   <item><see cref="RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory"/> — emits the role claims the area policies
///   match on (the single-generic default factory never does), plus the §3.5 obligation claims and
///   the display name;</item>
///   <item>security-stamp revalidation every 5 minutes, so resets, role revocations, and
///   deactivations bite live sessions within minutes (§3.1);</item>
///   <item>the area authorization policies (§3.7), via <see cref="AuthorizationServiceCollectionExtensions"/>;</item>
///   <item>the cascading <c>Task&lt;AuthenticationState&gt;</c> the Blazor router and
///   <c>AuthorizeView</c> consume;</item>
///   <item>the append-only <see cref="ISecurityEventLog"/> that sign-in outcomes are recorded to (§3.5).</item>
/// </list>
///
/// The obligations pipeline itself is enforced by <see cref="ObligationsMiddleware"/> in the request
/// pipeline (registered in <c>Program.cs</c>); the sign-in and forced-change pages are static-SSR
/// Razor components under <c>Components/Account</c>.
/// </summary>
public static class IdentityServiceCollectionExtensions
{
    /// <summary>The application authentication cookie name.</summary>
    private const string AuthenticationCookieName = "myrestaurant.authentication";

    /// <summary>Security-stamp revalidation interval (§3.1).</summary>
    private static readonly TimeSpan SecurityStampValidationInterval = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(5);

    /// <summary>Cookie sliding-expiration lifetime (§3.1).</summary>
    private static readonly TimeSpan AuthenticationCookieLifetime = TimeSpan.FromHours(24);

    public static IServiceCollection AddRestaurantIdentity(this IServiceCollection services, RestaurantOptions options)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(services);
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(options);

        // SignInManager and the cookie handlers resolve the current HttpContext.
        services.AddHttpContextAccessor();

        // Cookie authentication with Identity's four schemes (application, external, two-factor
        // remember-me, two-factor user-id). The application cookie is the default for
        // authenticate/challenge/forbid; the external cookie is the default sign-in scheme, matching
        // what AddIdentity configures internally (we compose it by hand to avoid a RoleManager).
        services.AddAuthentication(authentication =>
            {
                authentication.DefaultAuthenticateScheme = IdentityConstants.ApplicationScheme;
                authentication.DefaultChallengeScheme = IdentityConstants.ApplicationScheme;
                authentication.DefaultSignInScheme = IdentityConstants.ExternalScheme;
            })
            .AddIdentityCookies();

        // Area authorization policies (§3.7).
        services.AddRestaurantAuthorization();

        // The cascading Task<AuthenticationState> that <AuthorizeView>, AuthorizeRouteView, and the
        // account pages consume — in both static SSR and interactive-server rendering.
        services.AddCascadingAuthenticationState();

        services.AddIdentityCore<Person>(identity =>
            {
                // Usernames: 3–64 chars is enforced by the DB CHECK and citext handles uniqueness, so
                // do not additionally restrict the character set (empty = "allow any"); email is optional.
                identity.User.AllowedUserNameCharacters = string.Empty;
                identity.User.RequireUniqueEmail = false;

                // Password policy (§3.2): length 12, no composition rules, no expiry.
                identity.Password.RequiredLength = 12;
                identity.Password.RequiredUniqueChars = 1;
                identity.Password.RequireDigit = false;
                identity.Password.RequireLowercase = false;
                identity.Password.RequireUppercase = false;
                identity.Password.RequireNonAlphanumeric = false;

                // Lockout (§3.1): 5 consecutive failures lock for 5 minutes, applied to everyone.
                identity.Lockout.AllowedForNewUsers = true;
                identity.Lockout.MaxFailedAccessAttempts = 5;
                identity.Lockout.DefaultLockoutTimeSpan = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(5);

                // No email/phone confirmation gating (optional contact fields only, §11.1).
                identity.SignIn.RequireConfirmedAccount = false;
                identity.SignIn.RequireConfirmedEmail = false;
                identity.SignIn.RequireConfirmedPhoneNumber = false;

                // No personal-data protection layer over the store (we manage TOTP encryption ourselves).
                identity.Stores.ProtectPersonalData = false;
            })
            .AddUserStore<DapperUserStore>()
            .AddClaimsPrincipalFactory<RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory>()
            .AddDefaultTokenProviders() // Authenticator (TOTP), Data-Protection, email/phone token providers.
            .AddSignInManager<RestaurantSignInManager>();

        // AddIdentityCookies wires the application cookie's OnValidatePrincipal to the static
        // SecurityStampValidator, which resolves these at runtime. AddIdentityCore/AddSignInManager do
        // not register them (only the monolithic AddIdentity does), so register them explicitly.
        services.TryAddScoped<ISecurityStampValidator, SecurityStampValidator<Person>>();
        services.TryAddScoped<ITwoFactorSecurityStampValidator, TwoFactorSecurityStampValidator<Person>>();

        // Harden the application cookie (§3.1). Secure + HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax; 24-hour sliding
        // expiration. The login/logout/access-denied paths are the real account surfaces now (this
        // slice); an unauthenticated hit on an [Authorize] page redirects to /sign-in?ReturnUrl=….
        services.ConfigureApplicationCookie(cookie =>
        {
            cookie.Cookie.Name = AuthenticationCookieName;
            cookie.Cookie.HttpOnly = true;
            cookie.Cookie.SecurePolicy = CookieSecurePolicy.Always;
            cookie.Cookie.SameSite = SameSiteMode.Lax;
            cookie.ExpireTimeSpan = AuthenticationCookieLifetime;
            cookie.SlidingExpiration = true;
            cookie.LoginPath = AccountRoutes.SignIn;
            cookie.LogoutPath = AccountRoutes.SignOut;
            cookie.AccessDeniedPath = AccountRoutes.AccessDenied;
        });

        // Revalidate the security stamp every 5 minutes so administrative resets/revocations/
        // deactivations invalidate live sessions promptly (§3.1). Rebuilding the principal also
        // refreshes the role and obligation claims through the factory above.
        services.Configure<SecurityStampValidatorOptions>(validator =>
        {
            validator.ValidationInterval = SecurityStampValidationInterval;
        });

        // Replace Identity's PBKDF2 IPasswordHasher<Person> with Argon2id (§3.2). Singleton so the
        // process-wide concurrency semaphore genuinely bounds total concurrent hashes; the duration
        // hook feeds password_hash_duration_milliseconds (§12) without DataAccess knowing about metrics.
        services.Replace(ServiceDescriptor.Singleton<IPasswordHasher<Person>>(serviceProvider =>
        {
            RestaurantMetrics metrics = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<RestaurantMetrics>();
            return new Argon2idPasswordHasher(
                new Argon2HashingOptions(
                    options.Argon2MemoryKibibytes,
                    options.Argon2Iterations,
                    options.Argon2Parallelism,
                    options.Argon2MaxConcurrentHashes),
                metrics.RecordPasswordHashDuration);
        }));

        // The append-only security-event trail (§3.5, §3.7). Scoped, matching the Identity lifetime;
        // it holds no state and opens a connection per write from the singleton factory.
        services.AddScoped<ISecurityEventLog, DapperSecurityEventLog>();

        return services;
    }
}

# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/ObligationsEnforcement.cs
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---

  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/ObligationsEnforcement.cs
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/ObligationsEnforcement.cs
  Size:                  5.8 KiB (5937 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 08:43:37.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21654783
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               57d7313c15880c2dd2b084774d8c812c159afe07a65138d167e28b2a917335aa
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
using System.Security.Claims;
using MyRestaurant.Domain.Authentication;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Identity;

/// <summary>
/// The routes the post-authentication obligations pipeline (§3.5) and the account surfaces live on.
/// One place, so pages, the middleware, and the cookie configuration can never drift apart.
/// </summary>
public static class AccountRoutes
{
    /// <summary>The password sign-in page (static SSR so the cookie can be written).</summary>
    public const string SignIn = "/sign-in";

    /// <summary>The TOTP challenge on the password path (§3.4, §4.2).</summary>
    public const string SignInTwoFactor = "/sign-in/two-factor";

    /// <summary>A recovery code standing in for the TOTP code (§3.4).</summary>
    public const string SignInRecoveryCode = "/sign-in/recovery-code";

    /// <summary>The sign-out endpoint (POST only, antiforgery-protected).</summary>
    public const string SignOut = "/sign-out";

    /// <summary>Shown when an authenticated principal fails an area policy (§3.7).</summary>
    public const string AccessDenied = "/access-denied";

    /// <summary>The forced password-change page — obligation (1) of §3.5.</summary>
    public const string ForcedPasswordChange = "/account/change-password-required";

    /// <summary>The forced TOTP re-enrollment page — obligation (2) of §3.5.</summary>
    public const string ForcedTotpEnrollment = "/account/enroll-totp-required";
}

/// <summary>
/// The web-layer half of the §3.5 obligations pipeline. The <em>decision</em> is the pure, exhaustively
/// unit-tested <see cref="ObligationsPipeline"/> in the Domain; this class maps a
/// <see cref="ClaimsPrincipal"/>'s obligation claims onto that decision, says which request paths stay
/// reachable while an obligation is outstanding, and builds the redirect that sends the principal to
/// the right page. <see cref="ObligationsMiddleware"/> is a thin shell over these statics so the whole
/// enforcement surface is testable without a server.
/// </summary>
public static class ObligationsEnforcement
{
    /// <summary>
    /// The next outstanding obligation for <paramref name="principal"/>, decided by the Domain
    /// pipeline over the claims <see cref="RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory"/> issued at sign-in.
    /// </summary>
    public static PostAuthenticationObligation NextObligationFor(ClaimsPrincipal principal)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(principal);

        return ObligationsPipeline.NextObligation(
            HasObligationClaim(principal, RestaurantClaimTypes.MustChangePassword),
            HasObligationClaim(principal, RestaurantClaimTypes.MustEnrollTotp));
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// True when <paramref name="path"/> stays reachable while an obligation is outstanding (§3.5:
    /// "no authenticated endpoint except sign-out and the pipeline pages themselves"). Health probes
    /// and framework static assets are also exempt — they carry no user action. The Blazor circuit
    /// endpoint (<c>/_blazor</c>) is deliberately <b>not</b> exempt: while a flag is set, interactive
    /// circuits are refused too, so an already-open tab cannot keep acting.
    /// </summary>
    public static bool IsExemptPath(PathString path)
        => path.StartsWithSegments(AccountRoutes.ForcedPasswordChange)
        || path.StartsWithSegments(AccountRoutes.ForcedTotpEnrollment)
        || path.StartsWithSegments(AccountRoutes.SignOut)
        || path.StartsWithSegments(AccountRoutes.AccessDenied)
        || path.StartsWithSegments("/healthz")
        || path.StartsWithSegments("/_framework");

    /// <summary>The page that clears <paramref name="obligation"/>.</summary>
    public static string PageFor(PostAuthenticationObligation obligation) => obligation switch
    {
        PostAuthenticationObligation.ForcePasswordChange => AccountRoutes.ForcedPasswordChange,
        PostAuthenticationObligation.ForceTotpEnrollment => AccountRoutes.ForcedTotpEnrollment,
        PostAuthenticationObligation.None => throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(
            nameof(obligation), obligation, "No page exists when no obligation is outstanding."),
        _ => throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(nameof(obligation), obligation, "Unknown obligation."),
    };

    /// <summary>
    /// The redirect that sends the principal to the obligation page, carrying the originally
    /// requested URL so §3.5 step (3) — "continue to the originally requested URL" — can honour it
    /// once the pipeline clears.
    /// </summary>
    public static string RedirectTargetFor(PostAuthenticationObligation obligation, HttpRequest request)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(request);

        string destination = $"{request.PathBase}{request.Path}{request.QueryString}";
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(destination))
        {
            destination = "/";
        }

        return $"{PageFor(obligation)}?ReturnUrl={Uri.EscapeDataString(destination)}";
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Collapses a caller-supplied return URL to a safe local path: it must start with a single
    /// <c>'/'</c> (no scheme, no protocol-relative <c>//host</c>), otherwise <c>"/"</c> is returned.
    /// Shared by the sign-in pages and the sign-out endpoint so none of them can open-redirect.
    /// </summary>
    public static string SafeLocalReturnUrl(string? returnUrl)
    {
        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(returnUrl))
        {
            return "/";
        }

        bool isLocal = returnUrl[0] == '/'
            && !returnUrl.StartsWith("//", StringComparison.Ordinal)
            && !returnUrl.StartsWith("/\\", StringComparison.Ordinal);

        return isLocal ? returnUrl : "/";
    }

    private static bool HasObligationClaim(ClaimsPrincipal principal, string claimType)
        => string.Equals(principal.FindFirstValue(claimType), "true", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
}

--- METADATA ---

  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/ObligationsMiddleware.cs
  Size:                  2.3 KiB (2368 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 08:43:53.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21654781
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               646b031b14d67c2fb0aa3ebb38ca1765f8110423e73cede0e4e88d46a7d53102
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files

--- CONTENT ---
using System.Security.Claims;
using MyRestaurant.Domain.Authentication;

namespace MyRestaurant.WebApplication.Identity;

/// <summary>
/// Enforces the post-authentication obligations pipeline (TECHNICAL_SPECIFICATION §3.5, ADR-0010):
/// once a principal is authenticated, an outstanding <c>must_change_password</c> /
/// <c>must_enroll_totp</c> flag makes every endpoint unreachable except sign-out and the pipeline's
/// own pages — the request is redirected to the page that clears the next obligation, carrying the
/// original destination as <c>ReturnUrl</c>.
///
/// <para>The <em>decision</em> is the pure <see cref="ObligationsPipeline"/>; the claim mapping,
/// exemption list, and redirect construction are the testable statics on
/// <see cref="ObligationsEnforcement"/>; this class is only the pipeline shell. It sits after
/// authentication (it reads <see cref="HttpContext.User"/>) and before the endpoint runs. Anonymous
/// requests pass through untouched — the flags only exist on authenticated principals.</para>
///
/// <para>Two deliberate consequences: static files served by <c>UseStaticFiles</c> never reach this
/// middleware (it sits after them), and the Blazor circuit endpoint is <b>not</b> exempt — an
/// interactive tab left open when a flag lands cannot reconnect its circuit until the pipeline
/// clears, which is exactly the "nothing else reachable" the specification asks for.</para>
/// </summary>
public sealed class ObligationsMiddleware
{
    private readonly RequestDelegate _next;

    public ObligationsMiddleware(RequestDelegate next)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(next);
        _next = next;
    }

    public Task InvokeAsync(HttpContext context)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(context);

        ClaimsPrincipal user = context.User;
        if (user.Identity?.IsAuthenticated == true)
        {
            PostAuthenticationObligation obligation = ObligationsEnforcement.NextObligationFor(user);
            if (obligation != PostAuthenticationObligation.None
                && !ObligationsEnforcement.IsExemptPath(context.Request.Path))
            {
                context.Response.Redirect(ObligationsEnforcement.RedirectTargetFor(obligation, context.Request));
                return Task.CompletedTask;
            }
        }

        return _next(context);
    }
}

################################################################################
# FILE: src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory.cs
################################################################################

--- METADATA ---

  Relative path:         src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory.cs
  Absolute path:         /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/src/MyRestaurant.WebApplication/Identity/RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory.cs
  Size:                  3.8 KiB (3883 bytes)
  Last modified:         2026-07-19 08:43:10.000000000 -0400
  Permissions:           -rw-r--r--
  Owner:                 kushal:kushal
  Inode:                 21654778
  Hard links:            1
  MIME type:             text/plain
  SHA-256:               e3494a399852649400e3d00295e1f59c14854b93b134db4896d18b1c6eeef48d
  Last git commit:       56e1018 2026-07-19 16:39:47 -0400 add all files
